subject: All Americans, regardless of zip code, should have access to the American Dream
body:
Opportunity Zones are a key part of the tax cuts signed into law by President Donald J. Trump last December. Today, they got another huge boost.

https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2a919acdc7&e=3ee4985eea
The White House • December 12, 2018

** President Trump is lifting American communities that Washington forgot
------------------------------------------------------------
Opportunity Zones are a key part of the tax cuts signed into law by President Donald J. Trump last December. Today, they got another huge boost.

“For decades, job growth and investment have been concentrated in a few major metropolitan areas,” the President said after signing an executive order to create the White House Opportunity and Revitalization Council. “Some cities have thrived, while others have suffered chronic economic and social hardship. With Opportunity Zones, we are drawing investment into neglected and underserved communities of America.”

The purpose of Opportunity Zones is to lift up communities that have been neglected by Washington and left behind for far too long. The typical family income in these areas is 37 percent below their state’s median level. The average poverty rate is 32 percent—almost double the national average. Nearly 35 million Americans live in these communities. Now, help is on the way.

Here’s how it works: Tax relief is offered in exchange for long-term investments in the low-income communities that need it most. These poorer areas begin to see more job creation and lasting growth. This private investment fuels human potential in these communities, whereas government spending programs too often extinguish it.

The upshot: All Americans, regardless of zip code, should have access to the American Dream. With Opportunity Zones, we get closer to that reality.

President Trump is lifting up the communities that Washington forgot. (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ea69e4b901&e=3ee4985eea)

Watch: The President signs today’s executive order (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0a42f7b161&e=3ee4985eea)

** ‘We need to know who is coming in and going out’
------------------------------------------------------------
Yesterday’s tragic terror attack at a Christmas market in Strasbourg, France, reminds us that national security is nothing without border security.

Secretary of State Mike Pompeo explained the stakes. “This is an enormous challenge. We have this problem in the United States as well,” he said. “Lots of folks that we’re watching, we think are at risk, we try to monitor. But the numbers are so big. It’s why, frankly, border security matters.”

“We need to know who is coming in and out of our country.”

Controlling the border is a matter of public safety—one that includes many threats beyond terrorism. From October 2017 through this July, U.S. Border Patrol seized 6,423 pounds of cocaine, 532 pounds of heroin, 439,531 pounds of marijuana, 10,382 pounds of methamphetamine, and 332 pounds of fentanyl from Border Patrol checkpoints.

It’s time to make a deal. There's nothing partisan about protecting our borders to encourage legal immigration.

Throwback: When Sen. Chuck Schumer believed in securing our border (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c91d04929f&e=3ee4985eea)

Watch: President Trump host Democrat leaders in the Oval Office yesterday (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=37dbaf942e&e=3ee4985eea)

** Video of the Day
------------------------------------------------------------

https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=74d99dab98&e=3ee4985eea

For years, Democrats have acknowledged the need for border security—even voting for it in the past. Why won’t they come to the table in 2018?

For 60 years, the Delta Queen cruised the rivers of America, giving its passengers a unique experience of what it was like to travel America's waterways back in the 1800's. Then 10 years ago, it all came to a halt, and this beautiful queen was chained to a dock in Chattanooga, Tennessee. How did this happen? Thousands of mostly young males from Honduras, Guatemala, and El Salvador, have joined together in caravans and are trying to get into our country through our southern border. name: Jason Yates
subject: The fight worth having…
body:
| | And the game plan to win!
---
[ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66icYcIyRhB2URpXghNXmPGvPYQCjDAWlCe1QVMXDwooIyViWdKtv12G6tvAJ7yXP1Og&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181013com01&n=1)
| Peter, If you and I were to assess the problems our communities and our nation face today and trace them all the way back to their origins, I believe we would discover a majority – if not almost all of them - were created from a breakdown of the family. This realization can feel overwhelming like the problem is too big to solve, or it can empower you and me with the opportunity we have to affect change starting in our own homes with the people we love most. This [**article**](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66icYcIyRhB2URpXghNXmPGvPYQCjDAWlCe1QVMXDwooIyViWdKtv12G6tvAJ7yXP1Og&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181013com01&n=2) from our friend Pastor Jentezen Franklin, _Fight for your Family_ , was an encouragement to me and I hope it will be to you as well. _“Family is important to God. It was the very first institution established by God at the creation of man. Family is the foundation upon which the church, the nation, and all other God ordained institutions are built. In fact, the only thing to precede the home was God Himself. Satan’s number one goal is to divide, conquer, and destroy the home. If he can destroy the home and family, he can take down the church and ultimately the nation. Therefore, the best thing you can do for your family is fight, not with your family but for your family.”
_ – Pastor Jentezen Franklin Pastor Franklin outlines what it means to [**Fight for Your Family**](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66icYcIyRhB2URpXghNXmPGvPYQCjDAWlCe1QVMXDwooIyViWdKtv12G6tvAJ7yXP1Og&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181013com01&n=3); a great reminder for us all. And I know Christians all across the country are taking up this same fight! [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66icYcIyRhB2URpXghNXmPGvPYQCjDAWlCe1QVMXDwooIyViWdKtv12G6tvAJ7yXP1Og&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181013com01&n=4) **United. We Stand.** Jason Yates
CEO
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181013com01&n=5)
* * *
[Support My Faith Votes with your tax deductible donation.](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66idsHszZ_cNjdrkrk6Ct22R5LqpO-pYuvVIOOj4RsxM_Q&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181013com01&n=6) _My Faith Votes is a non-partisan movement focused on motivating Christians in America to participate in local and national elections and be active members of society. By partnering with local churches, pastors, and national faith leaders, My Faith Votes mobilizes and equips Christians to lead the conversation on the place of faith in culture and politics._

name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: It's time for action on gun safety
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress
body:
|
---
| | Team, On March 30, 1981, President Ronald Reagan was outside a hotel in Washington, DC when shots rang out. The President and several members of his staff were hit, including Press Secretary James Brady. Brady was shot in the head and initially pronounced dead at the hospital. Fortunately, Brady survived. He went on to become a staunch advocate for sensible gun safety. And in November of 1993, a law requiring a five-day waiting period for handgun purchases -- the Brady Bill -- was signed into law. These stories have become way too common. My friend and former colleague Congresswoman Gabby Giffords was a victim of gun violence too. And the problem continues to get worse. We now live in a country where our children fear gun violence in the classrooms and halls of their schools. The Brady Bill was a big step forward. But that was 25 years ago. **It's way past time for us to build on that effort.** [**Demand Congress pass common-sense gun safety legislation. Sign the petition >>**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113cc8/622b7b5b/1e4b4d90/307b7d06/1678759017/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/ja181211") Every day, 96 American lives end due to gun violence. Stories of gun violence are so frequent, it's easy to become desensitized. **But we must not accept this as the new normal.** The vast majority of Americans support common-sense gun safety measures. Thus far, Republicans in Congress have been too afraid of the NRA to move forward. It's time for that to change. [**Join us and demand action. Add your signature. Share. Tweet. Keep this issue on the front-burner.**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113cc8/622b7b5b/1e4b4d90/307b7d06/1678759017/VEsE/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/ja181211") Thank you. Debbie

name: Jay Inslee
subject: I don't know how to make this more clear
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
This is a dire warning -- but you've heard it from me.

| [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7ad70/2f5ea88d/7ab043fc/2cbdde2c/3168939203/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-fr-freeze-1214?utm_source=ema_2018-12-13_freeze-5&refcode=ema_2018-12-13_freeze-5&utm_campaign=freeze-5&utm_medium=ema") Friend, I don't know how to make this any more clear -- this legally-mandated fundraising freeze deadline is a make or break moment for our movement. Here's why I need your help. Just this week, global investors gave us a dire warning: If we don't drastically reduce carbon emissions and completely halt burning coal, we will face a global financial crash. **Look, we've known the threat of climate change for a long time.** But we also know how hard the fossil fuel special interests will fight tooth and nail against climate action. So here's the thing -- starting at midnight tomorrow, I won't be able to raise funds due to a state-mandated fundraising freeze. During the legislative session, I'll be fighting my hardest to push forward an agenda that provides real, clear solutions to climate change. But I need your help today to make sure we can keep our movement moving forward and fighting for progress during the legislative session. **That's why I'm asking you to become one of the 2,000 donors we need to reach our bold goal before m idnight tomorrow.** [**Will you help us hit our goal and become a pre-freeze donor to show your support right now?**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7ad70/2f5ea88d/7ab043fc/2cbdde2c/3168939203/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-fr-freeze-1214?utm_source=ema_2018-12-13_freeze-5&refcode=ema_2018-12-13_freeze-5&utm_campaign=freeze-5&utm_medium=ema") [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7ad70/2f5ea88d/7ab043fc/2cbdde2c/3168939203/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-fr-freeze-1214?utm_source=ema_2018-12-13_freeze-5&refcode=ema_2018-12-13_freeze-5&utm_campaign=freeze-5&utm_medium=ema") This week, I announced the boldest state-based climate agenda in the nation. Period. My plan includes a commitment to reach a 100% clean energy future so that by 2030, power utilities in our state will be fully carbon neutral. I run the state with the strongest economy in the country, so I know full-well that my agenda isn't just about saving our planet, it's also about creating jobs. So while plenty of politicians -- especially in D.C. -- love to talk about climate change, I'm going to use my role as governor to take real action. But I can't do it without you. I need your help today to keep our grassroots movement strong during this freeze and I need to do it now. Join me: [**https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-fr-freeze-1214**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7ad70/2f5ea88d/7ab043fc/2cbdde2c/3168939203/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-fr-freeze-1214?utm_source=ema_2018-12-13_freeze-5&refcode=ema_2018-12-13_freeze-5&utm_campaign=freeze-5&utm_medium=ema") I hope you know how serious I am about ending our dependence on fossil fuels forever. But I need your people power behind me to show them that we can -- and will -- accomplish it. clean_body: This is a dire warning -- but you've heard it from me. | "" Friend, I don't know how to make this any more clear -- this legally-mandated fundraising freeze deadline is a make or break moment for our movement. Here's why I need your help. Just this week, global investors gave us a dire warning: If we don't drastically reduce carbon emissions and completely halt burning coal, we will face a global financial crash. Look, we've known the threat of climate change for a long time. But we also know how hard the fossil fuel special interests will fight tooth and nail against climate action. So here's the thing -- starting at midnight tomorrow, I won't be able to raise funds due to a state-mandated fundraising freeze. During the legislative session, I'll be fighting my hardest to push forward an agenda that provides real, clear solutions to climate change. But I need your help today to make sure we can keep our movement moving forward and fighting for progress during the legislative session. That's why I'm asking you to become one of the 2,000 donors we need to reach our bold goal before m idnight tomorrow. "" "" This week, I announced the boldest state-based climate agenda in the nation. Period. My plan includes a commitment to reach a 100% clean energy future so that by 2030, power utilities in our state will be fully carbon neutral. I run the state with the strongest economy in the country, so I know full-well that my agenda isn't just about saving our planet, it's also about creating jobs. So while plenty of politicians -- especially in D.C. -- love to talk about climate change, I'm going to use my role as governor to take real action. But I can't do it without you. I need your help today to keep our grassroots movement strong during this freeze and I need to do it now. Join me: "" I hope you know how serious I am about ending our dependence on fossil fuels forever. But I need your people power behind me to show them that we can -- and will -- accomplish it. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | "" Now more than ever, we must support progressive leadership in America. Chip in to show you're with Jay Inslee as we fight for progress in Washington state and around the country._ | ""

subject: President Trump Signs Executive Order Promoting ‘Opportunity Zones’ in Distressed Towns
body:
"President Trump signed an executive order Wednesday to create a new White House council..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c7e89f3ba1&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **President Trump Signs Executive Order Promoting ‘Opportunity Zones’ in Distressed Towns**

“President Trump signed an executive order Wednesday to create a new White House council for promoting private investment in ‘opportunity zones’ in more than 8,700 distressed communities across the U.S., aiming to expand prosperity to neglected zip codes,” Dave Boyer reports for _The Washington Times_.

“Our goal is to ensure that America’s great new prosperity is broadly shared by all of our citizens,” President Trump said in the Roosevelt Room of the White House yesterday.

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7748797a70&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “With only a handful of legislative business days left on the books, we have one final chance to live up to the promise that we made to the parents, small business owners, blue collar workers, farmers, teachers, and ordinary people who helped us gain control in the first place. Simply put, it’s time to build the wall,” Rep. Jody Hice (R-GA) writes in the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d36a939606&e=3ee4985eea)_.
---
|
---
| “The Environmental Protection Agency announced Tuesday it is rolling back some of the excessive, and possibly illegal, water regulations imposed by the Obama administration. EPA’s announcement is a welcome relief for homeowners and property owners impacted by overly aggressive EPA officials,” Tim Huelskamp and James Taylor of the Heartland Institute write in _[The Hill](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fc3ab8a961&e=3ee4985eea)_.
---
|
---
| “The number of people arrested for illegally employing or working at U.S. companies skyrocketed more than 700 percent during President Trump's first full fiscal year in office,” Anna Giaritelli reports for the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6a626eee39&e=3ee4985eea)_. “An official with ICE's Homeland Security Investigations arm said the surge in arrests was the result of the Trump administration's changing the focus of the agency's mission to crack down on all illegal immigration.”
---
|
---
| “The Senate has agreed to a vote on a version of a bill supported by President Donald J. Trump, called the First Step Act. That bill is part of a criminal justice reform effort that includes steps by President Trump, through executive action, to reform our broken criminal justice system,” Corey Lewandowski writes in _[The Hill](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7717e619ba&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Conservatives support reforms that will help released prisoners to change their lives and become productive members of society.”
---
|

name: Tim Ryan
subject: Did you hear? We got $10.8 million for downtown Youngstown!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tim Ryan for Congress
body:
You may have seen the news, but last week I was thrilled to announce that Youngstown was awarded a $10.8 million BUILD Grant to improve our downtown. | |
---
**Folks: Last week I was thrilled to announce that Youngstown was awarded a $10.8 million BUILD Grant to improve our downtown! ** This grant will support a transformative infrastructure project that will upgrade and expand existing transportation systems to better connect sections of our community. [This project will not only create jobs in the short-term but also spur economic growth by making it easier for businesses to create jobs and attract customers. Learn more about this exciting new project and what it will bring for the people of Youngstown >>](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90eb/5486deaa/25e95300/dcf52c4/1146225457/VEsH/ "http://twitter.com/TimRyan/status/1071146464510918658") [](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90eb/5486deaa/25e95300/dcf52c7/1146225457/VEsE/ "https://twitter.com/TimRyan/status/1071146464510918658") It is clear that our nation faces many challenges due to failing infrastructure. The Army Corps of Engineers estimates we're overdue for $2 TRILLION in improvements. [When we invest in roads, bridges, and transit, we're doing more than creating jobs -- we're creating connected communities and jumpstarting our economy.](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90eb/5486deaa/25e95300/dcf52c7/1146225457/VEsF/ "https://twitter.com/TimRyan/status/1071146464510918658") My number one priority is improving lives and expanding opportunity for the working families of the 13th District, and I'm confident this latest investment will do just that. Thank you so much, \-- Tim

* * *

# Meet Tim Ryan
Tim Ryan is a champion for working families in Ohio's 13th District and is known for challenging both parties to do more to rebuild the middle class. Tim believes that every hardworking family deserves a good job, a quality education for their kids, affordable healthcare, and the security of knowing they can retire with dignity. Tim was elected to the U.S. House of Representatives in 2002 and has been fighting for working people ever since.
